# Phase 9.1.4 Evidence Traceability Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, or deployment authority.

## Objective

Standardize containment evidence, blocker evidence, unresolved-risk evidence, anti-activation proof, and survivability proof so future reviewers can understand why runtime remains blocked.

## Scope

- Containment evidence doctrine.
- Blocker traceability systems as documentary standards.
- Runtime blocker lineage doctrine.
- Fail-closed evidence standardization.
- Unresolved-risk evidence tracking.
- Governance skepticism evidence preservation.
- Anti-activation evidence doctrine.
- Constitutional blocker registry philosophy.
- Survivability proof packet standards.

## Required Safeguards

- No runtime activation exists.
- No provider execution exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No deployment authority exists.
- No execution-capable infrastructure exists.
- No evidence-review system creates runtime authority.
- No blocker-traceability layer bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No evidence-governance process enables hidden execution escalation.
- No survivability-proof layer creates infrastructure activation authority.
- No evidence-standardization framework weakens auditability.

## Step Improvement Notes

- Correct build order: follows fail-closed containment survivability review.
- Safe to include now: packet fields, blocker lineage, and anti-activation evidence standards.
- Future upgrade: blocker closure constraints and governance acceptance review.
- Do not include: executable registries, databases, workflows, providers, or runtime pathways.
